patch.value: pm deps tree must remain bounded on cyclic deep relationship graphs

During full-lineage orientation, pm deps pm-kr3t --format tree --json expanded until the process exhausted roughly 4 GB of memory. Tree rendering needs cycle-safe visitation, explicit node/depth/token bounds, and a compact truncation diagnostic so graph context remains safe for agents.

Cyclic and multiply-linked graphs terminate without repeated unbounded expansion; Tree output honors explicit node, depth, and token bounds with deterministic truncation metadata; A regression fixture reproduces the deep richly-linked topology without touching the real tracker; CLI and SDK dependency projections retain parity for bounded results

author: codex-root
message: Link bounded scale, context-budget, and relationship graph lineage

value.author: codex-root
value.text: Duplicate-check evidence: searched all-status tracker data for 'deps tree OOM memory', 'dependency graph cycle memory exhaustion', and 'deps format tree'. Existing pm-8kch covers missing-reference parity, pm-x85o the original visualization, pm-ju83 graph primitives, pm-9rxu scale, and pm-4k6b bounded context, but none tracks tree-format memory exhaustion. Reproduction observed during pm-kr3t orientation; the process grew to roughly 4 GB before termination. This item remains open and unclaimed because it was documented, not implemented, in the current SDK/digital-twin tranche.
